layer_map.what: `METHODS` const (7 methods, display order) + `HttpMethod = typeof METHODS[number]`; imports nothing renderer-external
re-point `RequestSpec.method` from `string` to `HttpMethod` imported from httpMethods; `makeBlankRequest` GET seed stays valid
add `updateActiveSpec(patch: Partial<RequestSpec>)`: resolve active tab internally, shallow-merge, set `dirty=true` only on actual change; existing lifecycle actions untouched

flat `RequestBar`: method Dropdown trigger + URL `<input>` + Send/Save/Share; per-field tabsStore selectors; `⌘↵`/`⌘S` document-keydown effect; `onSend` prop; token-bound semantic classes, zero inline styles
add `panes={{ request: <RequestBar onSend={…} /> }}` to the existing `<Shell tabs={<TabBar/>} />`; ToastProvider/Shell wiring otherwise unchanged
amend §4 sole-subscriber wording to admit RequestBar as the spec-edit subscriber (doc-only; no TabBar behavior change)

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: `onSend?: (intent: { tabId: string; method: HttpMethod; url: string }) => void`, default no-op; `HttpMethod` from httpMethods, all other fields primitives
key_design_decisions.why: Satisfies AC-13 (tab identity + method + url) WITHOUT importing `requestSpec` into a component — keeps §5.2 intact. Single path for Send click and ⌘↵.
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: Passing a `RequestSpec` object — forces a `requestSpec` import, violating §5.2. Passing only `(method,url)` — drops tab identity AC-13 requires.

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: Controlled `<input value={activeUrl} onChange={e ⇒ updateActiveSpec({ url: e.target.value })}>`; no remount key
key_design_decisions.why: Controlled binding swaps the displayed url on tab switch (AC-11) via the per-field selector; the url selector value is untouched on a method change, so React does not re-set the input value and caret/buffer are preserved (AC-18). Writes route through the store action (§4).
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: Uncontrolled + `defaultValue` — would not update on tab switch (AC-11). `key={activeTabId}` remount — needless churn; controlled binding already isolates per tab.

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: One `document` keydown effect handling both chords; reads live state via `tabsStore.getState()`; `e.preventDefault()` on ⌘S; cleanup on unmount
key_design_decisions.why: Mirrors Shell Effect 4 (document keydown + preventDefault + getState) — `getState()` avoids stale-closure capture so the handler acts on the current active tab regardless of focus (AC-16/AC-17).
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: React `onKeyDown` on a container — only fires when focus is inside RequestBar, failing "regardless of focus." Two separate effects — duplicate setup/teardown.

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: Single `canSend = url.trim() !== ''` derived once, consumed by the button `disabled`, the click handler, and the ⌘↵ handler
key_design_decisions.why: One predicate = one guard (DRY), so disabled state and the ⌘↵ path cannot diverge (AC-12/AC-13/AC-16 share trim semantics).
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: Computing the trim guard independently per call site — risks keyboard path and button drifting apart.

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: Local `useState` in RequestBar driving Dropdown's controlled `open`/`onOpenChange`
key_design_decisions.why: Dropdown (001) is a controlled-only Radix wrapper; local open-state is the contract. Reuses the existing molecule — no second overlay library (AC-3, §7).
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: Adding an uncontrolled/popover alternative — violates the reuse-Dropdown constraint and AC-3.

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: Per-field tabsStore selectors: `activeTabId`, active tab's `method`, `url`, `dirty` selected individually (mirror TabBar)
key_design_decisions.why: Selecting individual fields (not the whole `tabs` array) bounds re-renders to the values RequestBar shows, mitigating the §9 over-render risk; mirrors TabBar (§7).
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: Subscribing to the whole `tabs[]`/store object — re-renders on any unrelated tab mutation (§9 risk realized).

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: Trigger button carries one accessible name (visible method text serves as the name; no redundant `aria-label`), mirroring 005's pill aria approach
key_design_decisions.why: Resolves Q-1: single announcement, not doubled, matching the 005 precedent.
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: A separate `aria-label` over visible text — double-announces the method (the 005 pitfall).

key_design_decisions.chosen_approach: Reword "TabBar.tsx is the sole subscriber" → TabBar owns lifecycle wiring; RequestBar is the spec-edit subscriber. Doc-only.
key_design_decisions.why: DEPARTURE: introduces a 2nd tabsStore subscriber where §4 currently names TabBar the sole subscriber — sanctioned by AC-24, which mandates this exact amendment; TabBar runtime behavior is unchanged (AC-4).
key_design_decisions.alternatives_rejected: Leaving §4 as-is — leaves the constitution contradicting shipped code (AC-24 unmet).

file_impact.what_changes: `METHODS` const (GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE/OPTIONS/HEAD, display order) + `HttpMethod = typeof METHODS[number]`; pure const+type module
re-point `method: string` → `method: HttpMethod` (import from httpMethods); makeBlankRequest GET seed unchanged
add `updateActiveSpec(patch: Partial<RequestSpec>)` (resolve active tab, shallow-merge, dirty only on actual change); extend `TabsState` interface; existing actions untouched
the organism — method Dropdown + URL input + Send/Save/Share + ⌘↵/⌘S effect + `onSend` prop; per-field selectors
token-bound semantic classes for [method ▾][URL][Send/Save/Share] layout; horizontal URL scroll, no reflow
add `panes={{ request: <RequestBar onSend={…} /> }}` to the existing `<Shell tabs={<TabBar/>} />`
unit: trim guard, method-switch independence, Save dirty/no-op, per-tab render isolation
CT: layout, disabled Send, ⌘↵/⌘S, method-dropdown dismiss (two-step gate), per-tab isolation; fixtures import tokens.css + set data-mstyle
add `updateActiveSpec` cases: dirty-on-change, no-op-no-flip, per-tab isolation

doc_impact.what_changes: add RequestBar organism + httpMethods module to the renderer structure tree; note tabsStore gains updateActiveSpec

refresh the working-tabs pattern note (RequestBar is the spec-edit subscriber; updateActiveSpec) and correct the stale propless `<Shell/>` App-mount snippet to `<Shell tabs={<TabBar/>} panes={{request:…}} />`

risks.risk: RequestBar imports `requestSpec` to name the `RequestSpec` type, breaking §5.2 component-purity.
risks.mitigation: `onSend` typed with `HttpMethod` + primitives only; `updateActiveSpec` called with object literals (typed `Partial<RequestSpec>` on the store side). Lint/typecheck + a grep guard catch a stray import.

risks.risk: `httpMethods.ts` accidentally imports a store/component, breaking lib-leaf direction (§2.2).
risks.mitigation: Keep httpMethods a pure const+type module; `requestSpec`→`httpMethods` is the only inbound edge (lib→lib, downward).

risks.risk: `updateActiveSpec` over-dirties on a no-op patch, breaking the non-dirty Save no-op (AC-10/AC-15).
risks.mitigation: Set `dirty` only when a merged value actually differs; unit-test no-op-no-flip, change-flips, and per-tab isolation before UI wiring.

risks.risk: Narrowing `method` string→`HttpMethod` surfaces a hidden non-listed assignment elsewhere.
risks.mitigation: CBM-confirmed sole writer is `makeBlankRequest` (GET); lift the type into httpMethods and re-point in one change; typecheck/build gate (AC-5/AC-25/AC-27) catches strays.

risks.risk: CT flakiness from the Radix dismiss arm-race + missing `data-mstyle`/tokens context.
risks.mitigation: Apply the two-step dismiss gate (await animations + `setTimeout(0)` yield); CT fixtures import tokens.css and set `data-mstyle` on the host (005 CT lesson).

risks.risk: RequestBar over-renders as a 2nd tabsStore subscriber if it subscribes to the whole `tabs` array.
risks.mitigation: Per-field selectors for the active tab's method+url (mirror TabBar's memoized selector pattern).

risks.risk: Mount-point drift: docs/architecture.md shows a stale propless `<Shell/>` while App.tsx passes `tabs={<TabBar/>}`.
risks.mitigation: Plan §File Impact wires onto the verified App.tsx element; Documentation Impact refreshes the stale snippet at /finalize.

dependencies: None — no packages to install, no services or environment variables. Radix (`radix-ui`) and zustand are already in the project; the Dropdown molecule, Icon atom, `cx()`, and tokens.css all exist.
